svg.text:
┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ FCN ARCHITECTURE │ └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Traditional CNN (Classification): Input → [Conv layers] → [FC layers] → Class scores ↑ Fixed input size required
FCN (Segmentation): Input → [Conv layers] → [1×1 Conv] → [Upsample] → Pixel-wise scores ↑ ↑ Replace FC Restore resolution
FCN-8s Architecture: ────────────────────
Input: H×W×3 │ ▼ ┌─────────┐ │ VGG │ ← Pretrained backbone │ Conv1-5 │ └────┬────┘ │ ├──────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Pool3 (H/8) │ │ │ ├──────────────────────────┐ │ │ Pool4 (H/16) │ │ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ ┌─────────┐ │Conv6-7 │ 2× up │ Fuse │ 2× │ Fuse │ 8× up │(H/32) │ ──────────▶ │ Pool4 │ ──▶ │ Pool3 │ ────────▶ Output └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘ (H×W×C)
Why FCN-8s is better than FCN-32s: • FCN-32s: Single 32× upsample → coarse output • FCN-16s: Fuse pool4 → better boundaries • FCN-8s: Fuse pool3 + pool4 → fine details
